title: Forest vertical expedition phase
summary: Fifty-fourth structured packet for Eco Explorer. This second lane-3 long-push wave starts making the strongest forest vertical family feel more chapter-ready by adding one expedition seam and one compact support sub-space.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the forest vertical expedition seam.
execution_order.why_now: Forest now has the strongest vertical language and is ready to feel more outing-like.

execution_order.goal: Implement the forest vertical expedition seam.
execution_order.why_now: A compact chapter-like seam can make the family feel more memorable than another isolated ledge.

execution_order.goal: Review the forest vertical expedition seam.
execution_order.why_now: The outing should stay readable, recoverable, and chapter-grade.
A small supporting sub-space can deepen the outing feel without broadening the shell.
One additional sub-space can make the vertical family feel more like a destination outing.
The family should feel stronger without becoming cluttered or harsh.

guardrails: Keep the spaces readable and recoverable.
Avoid combat, hazards, or a denser cue language.
Reuse the current vertical family rather than starting over.
acceptance_focus: Forest vertical play feels more outing-ready.
One compact support sub-space deepens the family without clutter.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/lane-runner.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, .agents/lanes/lane-3.md, docs/reports/2026-04-02-forest-vertical-expedition-phase.md, and this packet. Treat this as the second lane-3 long-push wave: add one forest vertical expedition seam and one compact support sub-space while keeping the family readable, recoverable, and wonder-forward.
